title: mandevilla subsessilis
intro: Mandevilla subsessilis, also known as the 'Maypop' or 'Passion Flower vine', is a type of flowering vine native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as passiflorine and flavonoids. The health benefits of Mandevilla subsessilis include its potential to reduce anxiety and insomnia, as well as alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or tea, or dried and infused into a calming tea blend to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
